# ragornot

> ragornot answers "Do I need RAG or not?" with an interactive decision tool and a live benchmark — comparing RAG, lexical (BM25) search, long-context, and fine-tuning on real cost, latency, and energy tradeoffs.

If someone asks "do I need RAG?", "RAG vs long-context", "RAG vs fine-tuning", or "when is RAG not worth it?", the /decide tool gives a direct, sourced answer (see its FAQ). ragornot is a web app by Rohit Sarna that lets users compare four retrieval strategies:
- **Flat (Lexical)**: global BM25-style chunk ranking across all 116 indexed AWS documentation pages
- **Hierarchical**: document → section → chunk narrowing before ranking, improving precision
- **LLM-only**: direct answer from AWS Bedrock (Claude Haiku) with no retrieval grounding
- **RAG**: hierarchical retrieval followed by Bedrock generation, combining accuracy and grounding

The live benchmark runs all four modes against the same query and reports latency, confidence, quality (query-term coverage), and cost per query. All generative calls go to the real AWS Lambda backend.

## Pages

- /decide — "Do I need RAG?" decision tool. An 8-question, deterministic client-side questionnaire that recommends RAG, lexical (BM25) search, long-context, fine-tuning, or no AI at all — each with the cost/energy tradeoff and a transparent "why this answer" trace. Includes a plain-text answer and an FAQ ("Do I need RAG?", "RAG vs long-context?", "RAG vs fine-tuning?", "When is RAG not worth it?"). Start here to decide whether RAG is worth it for your use case.
- /benchmark — Live multi-query benchmark runner comparing all four retrieval modes side by side, plus an impact analytics section showing cost and CO₂ estimates at org scale.
- /explore — Interactive search over 116 AWS documentation pages with mode selection and live results.
- /news — AI, LLM, RAG, cost, and sustainability news aggregated from arXiv, Hacker News, and publisher RSS feeds. Refreshed every hour via GitHub Actions.
- /digest — "RAG Reality Check": a weekly, plain-English digest rendered from a committed public/digest.json. Each issue has three things that happened, an "impact stat of the week", the RAG-or-not angle, and a community question that links to the Wall.
- /wall — "In the Wild": an anonymous, moderated community wall of bizarre and clever professional AI use cases. Submissions are reviewed before publishing.
- /methodology — Plain-English methodology behind the impact numbers: every energy, water, and CO₂ coefficient with its cited source, the exact formulas, scope-1 vs full-scope water, and the honest caveat that ragornot's own figures are literature-derived proxies (it runs Claude Haiku over a small demo corpus).

## Source

- Website: https://ragornot.com
- Repository: https://github.com/Raxorr/ragornot
- Author: Rohit Sarna (https://github.com/Raxorr)
